note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. sec-iac owns Infrastructure-as-Code security review (Terraform, CloudFormation, Pulumi, K8s manifests, Helm, Dockerfiles, docker-compose): IAM over-permissioning, exposed endpoints, weak encryption, network boundaries, privileged/root containers. Negatives are near-misses that share infra/security vocabulary but belong to a sibling (sec-ci, sec-secrets, sec-deps, sec-scan, sec-gate, sec-threat-model, sec-fix) or a plain explain/migration-plan/edit action.

queries.text: can you review our Terraform for security issues before we apply?
scan the Dockerfile — it runs as root and I don't think we set a read-only rootfs
our infra/main.tf opens a security group to 0.0.0.0/0 on 22 — do an IaC review of the whole module
harden our kubernetes cluster — the pods have no securityContext and one deployment sets privileged: true
manager asked me to check the infrastructure for over-permissioned IAM before we promote the module to prod
review k8s/*-deployment.yaml — no resource limits and the service account looks way too broad
check our helm chart and values.yaml, worried the S3 bucket isn't encrypted at rest and public access isn't blocked
docker-compose review pls — one service mounts /var/run/docker.sock and another binds a db to 0.0.0.0
before deploying this CloudFormation stack, sanity-check the RDS instance isn't publicly_accessible and has encryption on
iac review of our pulumi stack — I think a couple of resources are in the default VPC and use unpinned base images
audit our .github/workflows for supply chain risk — actions aren't SHA-pinned and GITHUB_TOKEN is write-all
queries.note: CI/CD pipeline config hardening → sec-ci, not the infra it provisions

queries.text: scan the repo and git history for a leaked AWS access key someone may have committed in a tfvars file
queries.note: leaked-secret detection across code+history → sec-secrets even though a tfvars file is mentioned

queries.text: audit the container image's packages for known CVEs and check the go.mod for vulnerable modules
queries.note: dependency/CVE audit of shipped packages → sec-deps, not IaC config review

queries.text: explain how our Terraform networking module wires the VPC, subnets, and NAT gateway together
queries.note: understanding what the infra does → explain, not a security review

queries.text: plan the migration from our hand-rolled CloudFormation to Terraform with a rollback strategy
queries.note: migration planning → migration-plan, not a security scan

queries.text: quick security check on my staged changes before I commit this new Dockerfile
queries.note: fast diff-scoped pre-commit gate → sec-gate, not a full IaC review

queries.text: write me a Terraform module that provisions an encrypted RDS instance in a private subnet
queries.note: authoring new infra → plain edit task, not reviewing existing IaC

queries.text: full OWASP Top 10 audit of the whole service — code, secrets, deps, and infra — before we ship
queries.note: comprehensive whole-codebase audit that orchestrates the sub-scans → sec-scan

queries.text: do a STRIDE threat model of our new multi-tenant deployment and its trust boundaries
queries.note: STRIDE threat modeling → sec-threat-model, not a config misconfiguration scan

queries.text: tfsec flagged an unencrypted EBS volume in our module — generate the fix and a test to prove it
queries.note: remediating an already-found finding into a patch → sec-fix, not the scan itself

queries.text: our deploy pipeline provisions Terraform — review the infrastructure it stands up for exposed endpoints and weak encryption
queries.note: a pipeline is mentioned but the target is the provisioned infra config → must beat sec-ci

queries.text: check our Kubernetes manifests — the dashboard is on a LoadBalancer with no auth and pods run privileged
queries.note: scoped to K8s manifest misconfig, not a whole-codebase audit → must beat sec-scan

queries.text: did we ever commit a hardcoded password in a Dockerfile or compose file? scan the repo and history
queries.note: detecting leaked secrets across code+history is sec-secrets' job even though IaC files are named

queries.text: harden our GitHub Actions workflow that builds and pushes the Docker image — pin the actions and scope the token
queries.note: Docker appears but the target is the CI/CD workflow config itself → sec-ci wins
